assessment_items.question_content:
What was Germany's main justification for unrestricted submarine warfare in World War I?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Britain's blockade of Germany in the North Sea had a large impact on the Central powers ability to import goods, especially food.  Germany had a weaker surface fleet than the British and it was being confined to a corner of the North Sea off their coast.  The only way that they could attempt to retaliate with a blockade of the British Isles was through unrestricted submarine warfare.  The main risk, however, was that this would provoke the United States into entering the War on the side of the Allies.

This is covered in [this video](/humanities/history/euro-hist/american-entry-world-war-I/v/zimmermann-telegram).

choices.content: It wanted to goad the United States into entering the war
It wanted to kill as many civilians as possible
Britain's blockade of Germany was crippling and this was the only way to attempt to blockade Britain
Submarines were safer than ships
It was the only way to defeat the Russians

assessment_items.question_content:
True or False: Passengers on the Lusitania had no idea that it could possibly be attacked by a German u-boat.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
False.  The German embassy, in fact, took a newspaper advertisement out in New York to warn passengers that British ships--military or passenger--might be attacked at sea.

This is covered in [this video](/humanities/history/euro-hist/american-entry-world-war-I/v/blockades--u-boats-and-sinking-of-the-lusitania).

choices.content: true
false

assessment_items.question_content:
Other than Germany's use of unrestricted submarine warfare, what other incident angered Americans and helped catalyze American entry into World War I?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is covered in [this video on the Zimmerman Telegram](/humanities/history/euro-hist/american-entry-world-war-I/v/zimmermann-telegram).

In the Zimmermann Telegram (January, 1917), Germany outlined to Mexico how it would be likely that the U.S. would enter the War on the side of the Entente as soon as Germany resumed unrestricted submarine warfare (which it planned on doing the next month).  They go on to offer, given the above assumptions, support for the Mexicans to declare war on the United States in the event of American entry into the War.  This telegram was intercepted by British Intelligence and shared with the Americans.  This telegram and Germany's attempt to stir up Mexico against the United States was directly cited in President Wilson's war message to Congress on April 2, 1917.
Germany did not attack any American naval bases nor was its treatment of ethnic minorities of major note during World War I.

Germany did commit atrocities in Belgium at the outbreak of war in 1914 which engendered sympathy for the Entente among Americans, but this did not directly lead to American involvement in the War (which did not occur until 1917).

choices.content: Atrocities committed by Germany in Belgium
German attempt to persuade Mexico to declare war on the United States
German attack on American naval bases in the Pacific
German use of poison gas on the Western Front
Germany's treatment of ethnic minorities

assessment_items.question_content:
True or False:  The British naval blockade of Germany in World War I had only a minor effect on Germany's people and economy.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
False. The British blockade of Germany during World War I is considered to be a key element for the eventual Allied victory in 1918.  It dramatically reduced the Central Powers' ability to import raw materials and munitions. It also caused food rationing and malnutrition among the civilian population of Germany (and Austria-Hungary). 

It is covered in [this video](/humanities/history/euro-hist/american-entry-world-war-I/v/blockades--u-boats-and-sinking-of-the-lusitania).

choices.content: true
false

assessment_items.question_content:
What was Woodrow Wilson's winning slogan in the U.S. presidential election of 1916?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
In 1916, the American people, for the most part, were still against entering World War I (despite the fact that the Lusitania was sunk in 1915). Woodrow Wilson capitalized on this by running for reelection with the slogan "He kept us out of war."

This is covered in [this video](/humanities/history/euro-hist/american-entry-world-war-I/v/united-states-enters-world-war-i).

choices.content: Make the world safe for democracy
He kept us out of war
Remember the Lusitania
Fourteen points to war
Prosperity and progress

assessment_items.question_content:
Which of the following is **not** true about the sinking of the Lusitania?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This topic is covered in [this video](/humanities/history/euro-hist/american-entry-world-war-I/v/blockades--u-boats-and-sinking-of-the-lusitania).

The sinking of the Lusitania angered Americans, but did not directly lead to American entry into the War.  This did not happen until nearly two years later (in 1917) after the resumption of German unrestricted submarine warfare and the Zimmerman Telegram.

choices.content: Germany took an advertisement warning Americans that British ships could be destoyed
It definitely carried some military cargo
Its sinking in 1915 angered America and Germany stopped unrestricted submarine warfare on passenger liners later that year
It directly caused America to enter World War I against the Germans
It was sunk by a German u-boat off the coast of Ireland as part of a blockade of the British Isles.

assessment_items.question_content:
What type of naval vessel allowed the Germans to not be completely dominated by the British at sea during World War I?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Covered in [this video](/humanities/history/euro-hist/american-entry-world-war-I/v/blockades--u-boats-and-sinking-of-the-lusitania)

The British had the dominant surface fleet in the world entering into World War I.  Germany's surface fleet was mainly relegated to a corner of the North Sea during the war.  What allowed them to attempt to blockade the British was their use of submarines (U-boats).

choices.content: aircraft carriers
destroyers
submarines
battle cruisers
zepplins

assessment_items.question_content:
What is **not** true about the Zimmerman telegram?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Even though the Zimmerman Telegram tried to convince the Mexicans to declare war on the United States if the U.S. were to enter World War I (arguably to redirect American resources from Europe), the Mexicans didn't take it seriously and never mobilized their troops.

This is covered in [this video](/humanities/history/euro-hist/american-entry-world-war-I/v/zimmermann-telegram)

choices.content: It is named after the German Foreign Minister that sent it
It made clear Germany's intention to resume unrestricted submarine warfare
It tried to convince the Mexicans to declare War on the United States if the Americans entered WWI
It caused the Mexicans to mobilize troops on the U.S. border
It angered the Americans and was a major catalyst for U.S. entry into WWI

assessment_items.question_content:
Which of the following is **not** true of the Americans' attitude and involvement in World War I in late 1914 and 1915?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Americans were upset about German atrocities in Belgium and were angered by the sinking of the Lusitania.  They also maintained close trade ties with Great Britain.  None of this, however, was enough to convince the United States that it shouldn't be neutral.  Woodrow Wilson would in fact win the 1916 presidential election based on a neutrality platform.  

This is mainly covered in [this video](/humanities/history/euro-hist/american-entry-world-war-I/v/blockades--u-boats-and-sinking-of-the-lusitania).

choices.content: They didn't want to get involved in a European conflict
They were eager to come to the defense of their close ally Great Britain
They maintained close economic and trade ties with Great Britain
They were upset about the atrocities committed by Germany in Belgium
They were angered by the sinking of the Lusitania in 1915
